evidence: Terms are built using variables, constants, and function symbols (e.g., FatherOf(X))
Predicates are then built by applying relations to these terms (e.g., Tall(FatherOf(Bill)))
Variables are terms. Constants are terms. If t_1,...,t_n are terms and f is a function symbol... then f(t_1,...,t_n) is also a term
If t_1,...,t_n are terms and p is a relation symbol... then p(t_1,...,t_n) is a predicate
